tasks.description: Dynamic page titles — add generateMetadata to workspace layout (template pattern) and all page.tsx files so each page has a unique <title> satisfying WCAG 2.4.2. Pattern: '[Page Name] — [Workspace Name] — Stride'. Auth pages get static titles. /workspaces gets 'Workspaces — Stride'. Settings page (client component) uses layout default.
acceptance_criteria: Root layout.tsx title becomes template: { template: '%s — Stride', default: 'Stride — Process Mapping & Continuous Improvement' }
[workspaceId]/layout.tsx exports generateMetadata with title: { template: '%s — {workspaceName} — Stride', default: '{workspaceName} — Stride' }
All 14 server-component page.tsx files under [workspaceId]/ export generateMetadata or static metadata with page-specific title (e.g. 'Settings', 'Dashboard', 'Activity')
Canvas [tabId]/page.tsx uses tab name as title (dynamic via generateMetadata)
/login and /signup pages export metadata with title 'Log In' and 'Sign Up'
TypeScript compilation passes with zero new errors
No duplicate title across any two different pages

patterns_to_follow: public/[shareId]/page.tsx uses generateMetadata with Supabase query to get workspace name
Next.js title.template in layout — child page title strings get interpolated into template
Root layout already has static metadata export at line 21
learnings_relevant: NEXT_PUBLIC_ static replacement — not relevant here but be careful with env vars
Settings page.tsx is 'use client' — cannot export metadata/generateMetadata. Layout default will apply.

tasks.special_instructions: The [workspaceId]/layout.tsx already fetches workspace. Add generateMetadata that does the same query (Next.js deduplicates). Use title.template: '%s — {workspaceName} — Stride'. For settings/page.tsx which is 'use client', do NOT add metadata — the layout default '{workspaceName} — Stride' is acceptable. For [tabId]/page.tsx, use the tab name from the existing query. Also resolves IMP-108.

tasks.description: Show info note in export PDF dialog when a preset is applied but some sections are masked by availability. E.g. 'N sections unavailable — add data to unlock them'.
acceptance_criteria: When a preset is applied and availability masks >= 1 section, a muted info note appears below the preset buttons
Note shows count of masked sections (e.g. '3 sections unavailable — add data to unlock them')
Note disappears when no preset is active or all preset sections are available
Styling matches existing muted text pattern (text-white/55, text-xs)

patterns_to_follow: maskWithAvailability at line 77 already computes masked config
availability prop is Record<keyof ExportConfig, boolean>

tasks.special_instructions: Compute masked count by comparing preset config keys (where preset[k]=true) against availability[k]. Show note only when activePreset !== 'custom' and maskedCount > 0.

tasks.description: Add Analysis toggle button to the tools canvas toolbar alongside 'Add Tool' and 'Add Tool Section' buttons, so users can toggle analysis view from the toolbar without needing the sidebar.
acceptance_criteria: An 'Analysis' button appears in the tools canvas toolbar (alongside Add Tool, Add Tool Section)
Clicking toggles showAnalysis state (same as existing sidebar toggle)
Button text switches between 'Analysis' and 'Canvas' based on showAnalysis state
Existing sidebar analysis toggle continues to work

patterns_to_follow: handleToggleAnalysis callback at line 311 already exists
Toolbar area with Add Tool / Add Tool Section buttons — find in JSX
Existing sidebar analysis toggle at line 567 uses same handler

tasks.special_instructions: Reuse the existing handleToggleAnalysis callback. Match button styling to other toolbar buttons. Use BarChart3 or similar icon from lucide-react.

warnings_added: IMP-107, IMP-109, IMP-110 were resolved by BUG-045/046/047 in iter 132 — should be marked done by reviewer
